overview.Title: A Cyber Attack on Water Infrastructure by Juicy Mix
overview.Summary: The Juicy Mix campaign targets water and wastewater systems using a multi-stage attack involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: Gathering information about the target's infrastructure and identifying potential entry points using OSINT on publicly accessible cloud assets.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 673 - Developer Signing Maliciously Altered Software
stage.description: Developing a simple yet effective payload tailored for SCADA systems used in water and wastewater management.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Delivering malware via phishing emails targeting employees with access to the water system networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: Exploiting web-based interfaces of SCADA systems to gain initial access to the system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 579 - Replace Winlogon Helper DLL
stage.description: Creating persistent registry entries or startup folder items to ensure the malware survives reboots.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Using known vulnerabilities in SCADA software to escalate privileges to administrative levels.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 448 - Embed Virus into DLL
stage.description: Temporarily disabling antivirus software to avoid detection while executing subsequent stages of the attack.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 226 - Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: Extracting credentials from memory or other storage locations to facilitate lateral movement.

attack_flow.stage: Network Service Discovery <surface/Internal network Network scanning N/A CAPEC ID: 309 - Network Topology Mapping Mapping out the internal network to identify all connected devices and services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 555 - Remote Services with Stolen Credentials
stage.description: Using RDP to move laterally between systems within the water and wastewater network.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Collecting sensitive information related to water treatment processes and operational data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Sending collected data back to the attacker's command and control server via email.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Tampering with SCADA controls to disrupt water supply or treatment processes, potentially causing environmental harm.

Scenario.summary: The Juicy Mix campaign successfully infiltrates a water and wastewater system through a series of sophisticated steps, ultimately leading to significant disruption of service. The attackers leverage multiple tactics, including reconnaissance, resource development, and targeted phishing, to establish a foothold and escalate privileges. They persistently maintain access, evade defenses, collect sensitive data, and exfiltrate information before finally causing operational disruptions that endanger public safety.
